jobPostingInfo.title: Data Insights Analyst - Claims
jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
You have a clear vision of where your career can go. And we have the leadership to help you get there. At CNA, we strive to create a culture in which people know they matter and are part of something important, ensuring the abilities of all employees are used to their fullest potential.

Join an analytics team within the Property & Casualty Claims department that helps bring data to life through visualizations and analysis. In this role, you’ll partner directly with Claims leaders to understand business needs and deliver analytics, dashboards, and data solutions that influence operational decisions and strategic initiatives.

Ideal candidates are eager to learn Looker, SQL, insurance analytics, and enterprise data processes in a collaborative team environment. We are seeking someone with a curiosity and desire to learn traditional analytics and explore AI enabled analytics.

This position enjoys a flexible, hybrid work schedule of 2 days per week in our Chicago, IL office.

JOB DESCRIPTION:

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

Performs a combination of duties in accordance with departmental guidelines:

  • Performs basic modeling for the purpose of quantifying the performance of the business area and evaluating operational changes. Reports findings to consultants/senior consultants and proposes preliminary recommendations based on insights.
  • Interprets data and identifies relevant analyses and possible solutions using judgment and precedents.
  • Assists with the building of dashboards (using excel and/or Proprietary Software) and helps with the preparation of reports to Senior Consultants and/or Management.
  • Provides quantitative support to interpret and/or model data and explains variances/trends.
  • Responds to and fulfills adhoc data requests.


Reporting Relationship
Typically Manager or above.

Skills, Knowledge and Abilities

  • Knowledge of financial, economic, and/or statistical practices, procedures and principles, which may include a specialty area of the business
  • Developing technical expertise and product knowledge.
  • Good analytical and problem solving skills with the ability to effectively resolve simple to moderately complex situations and issues. Demonstrates attention to detail.
  • Good interpersonal and communication skills. Ability to effectively interact with all levels of CNA's internal and external business partners.
  • Collaborates with all levels of team members to explain potentially complex information.
  • Good time management and strong organizations skills with the ability to prioritize and complete work and/or projects effectively.
  • Ability to exercise independent judgment and make sound business decisions effectively
  • Ability to adapt to change and value diverse opinions and ideas.
  • Has a strong knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite and other business-related software.


Education and Experience

  • Bachelor's Degree in Business, Economics, Mathematics, Finance, Statistics, or related field.
  • Typically up to two years of related work experience.
  • Experience with SQL, Looker and/or data visualization tools, a plus.

#LI-KP1

#LI-Hybrid

In certain jurisdictions, CNA is legally required to include a reasonable estimate of the compensation for this role. In District of Columbia, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, New York and Washington, the national base pay range for this job level is $47,000 to $78,000 annually. Salary determinations are based on various factors, including but not limited to, relevant work experience, skills, certifications and location. CNA offers a comprehensive and competitive benefits package to help our employees – and their family members – achieve their physical, financial, emotional and social wellbeing goals. For a detailed look at CNA’s benefits, please visit cnabenefits.com.

CNA is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to qualified individuals with disabilities in the recruitment process. To request an accommodation, please contact leaveadministration@cna.com.
